content.text:
Here is what changed.

### 1. `HeaderUserInfo` / `AppHeader` (`packages/ui/src/components/app-header.tsx`)

`HeaderUserInfo` now supports optional:

- **`profileHref` / `profileLabel`** — Renders a **Mijn profiel** row with `UserIcon`. Uses `NavLink` when the header gets `NavLink`, otherwise a plain `<a>`.
- **`company`** — `{ name, description? }` — Renders an **Organisatie** block under the user lines (name + optional second line).

Both **desktop** (`DropdownMenu`) and **mobile** (`Sheet`) menus include the org block and profile entry. Extra separators separate profile from language where needed.

### 2. `AuthenticatedAppShell`

For a real session, the header user payload now includes:

- **`profileHref`** — From `PROTOTYPE_PRIMARY_NAV` (`user-profile` → `/app/user-profile`), with **`profileLabel`**: `"Mijn profiel"`.
- **`company`** — **`name`**: `user.homeOrganization.name` (company you’re logged in as). **`description`**: `Vertegenwoordigt: …` only when `representedOrganization.id !== homeOrganization.id`.

Also corrected the first breadcrumb link from **`/dashboard`** to **`/requests`** so “Workspace” matches the real default route.

The onboarding app **`bun run build`** succeeds with these updates. (`packages/ui`’s own `tsc` still reports unrelated story/sewdn issues that were already there.)

content.text:
Here is what was added and how it behaves.

### `HeaderUserInfo.company` (`packages/ui/src/components/app-header.tsx`)

- **`organizationProfileHref` / `organizationProfileLabel`** — Renders an **Organisatieprofiel** row with `CubeIcon` (same idea as the sidebar), using `NavLink` when the header provides `NavLink`.
- **`organizationSwitcher`** — Optional `{ organizations, activeOrganizationId, onSelectOrganization }`. If `organizations.length > 1`, the menu shows **Wissel van organisatie**:
  - **Desktop:** submenu with `DropdownMenuRadioGroup` + `Building02Icon` on the trigger.
  - **Mobile:** labeled list of buttons; active row shows `Tick01Icon`.

Separators before the language block run when any of user profile, org profile, or multi-org switcher is present.

### Mock session & auth (`ui-pt1-prototype`)

- **`MockPrototypeUser.organizations`** — Optional list of orgs the account can use (deduped by `id`). If omitted, behaviour is unchanged: only `homeOrganization`.
- **`MockPrototypeSession`** — Now includes **`activeOrganization`** and **`organizations`** (resolved list).
- **`mockPrototypeMembershipsForUser()`** — Exported helper to build that list (dedupe, default to home).
- **`MockPrototypeAuthProvider`** — Persists `{ userId, activeOrganizationId? }` in the same sessionStorage key; resolves `activeOrganization`; validates stored org id when the user or list changes.
- **`setActiveOrganization`** on context + **`useMockPrototypeSetActiveOrganization()`** hook (also available via **`useMockPrototypeAuth()`**).

### Onboarding app

- **`mock-prototype-users.ts`** — **Jane** has `organizations: [PROCERTUS, Acme]` so you can try switching.
- **`AuthenticatedAppShell`** — Sidebar workspace uses **`session.activeOrganization`**. Header company name / “Vertegenwoordigt” line uses **active** vs **represented** org ids. Passes profile URL, org profile URL from `PROTOTYPE_PRIMARY_NAV` (`organization-profile`), and the switcher when `session.organizations.length > 1`.
- **`reviewRequesterFromSession`** — “Workspace:” line uses **`session.activeOrganization.name`** when present.

Signing in as **Jane Doe** gives **Mijn profiel**, **Organisatieprofiel**, and **Wissel van organisatie** between PROCERTUS and Acme; the sidebar workspace name updates with the selection. Other demo users stay single-org (no switcher).

content.text:
Here is what was added.

### Route
- `/dashboard` now renders **`DashboardPage`** instead of the placeholder (`App.tsx`).

### Files
1. **`src/pages/dashboard-mock-data.ts`** — Static mock strings and numbers (user, org, requests, certificates, user management, orders, invoices, demo snippets).
2. **`src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx`** — Full layout:
   - **Welcome hero** (full width): gradient background, Dutch welcome copy, and a **large** `/Procertus Logo with tagline.svg` in a frosted panel (`h-32` → `h-48` on large screens).
   - **12-column bento grid** with section labels aligned to the sidebar:
     - **Hoofdmenu**: Dashboard KPIs (4 tiles), **Mijn profiel** (mock Jane Doe), **Gebruikersbeheer** (12 users / 4 roles + role chips), **Organisatieprofiel** (mock PROCERTUS + VAT + **recente certificatie-aanvragen** list with status badges).
     - **Certificatie**: **Aanvragen** (badge summary), **Certificaten & Attesten** (counts + last document line).
     - **Financieel**: **Bestellingen**, **Facturen**.
     - **Demo**: **Organisatie**, **Beslissingsboom**, **Design system**.
     - **Onderaan in de zijbalk**: compact links for **Help** and **Settings** from `PROTOTYPE_SECONDARY_NAV` (same URLs as the sidebar, `#` for now).

Each main bento tile uses the **same icon and title** as `navConfig` and an **Openen →** link to the matching route via `navItemByKey`.

### Design notes
- Cards share a light **hover** (border + shadow), icon in a **primary-tinted** rounded square, and responsive **column spans** (`col-span-12` with `lg:col-span-*`) for a bento-style rhythm.
- All metrics and lists are **fixed mocks**; nothing reads session or API data.

If you want the hero to use the vertical logo asset or a different tagline file, say which file under `public/` you prefer.

### Main region height & scrolling

- The layout root is `flex … overflow-hidden`; the **main slot** (`.detail-panels-main-view`) is a **`flex h-full min-h-0 flex-col overflow-hidden`** column so children receive a bounded height.
- **Do not** force `100svh` on content inside the main slot unless the app root is only this layout. Prefer **`h-full min-h-0`** so the shell fills the measured panel width/height.

content.text:
Here is what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause
1. **`ManagementAppShell`** used **`h-svh`** on `SidebarProvider`, so the shell always tried to be **viewport** height even when it lives inside **`PanelsLayout`’s main column** (which has a **smaller** width/height budget next to docked panels). That breaks the usual `min-h-0` → **bounded flex child** chain.
2. **`<main>`** used **`overflow-hidden`**, so tall routes never scrolled inside the shell; with `html/body/#root { overflow: hidden }` in globals, **nothing** could scroll.
3. The header “scrolled” shadow listened to **`window.scrollY`**, but scroll is **not** on the window in this setup, so that signal was wrong (secondary issue).

### Fixes

**`packages/ui/src/components/app-shell/management-app-shell.tsx`**
- `SidebarProvider`: **`h-svh` → `h-full min-h-0 w-full max-h-full overflow-hidden`** so the shell **fills the panels main slot** and participates in flex shrink correctly.
- **`<main>`**: **`overflow-hidden` → `overflow-y-auto overflow-x-hidden`** so **route content scrolls inside `<main>`** (with `min-h-0 flex-1` unchanged).
- Scroll listener: **`ref` on `<main>`** and **`scrollTop`** instead of `window.scrollY`.

**`packages/ui/src/components/sewdn/Panels/Panels.tsx`**
- **Main slot** (`.detail-panels-main-view`): **`h-full overflow-auto` → `flex h-full min-h-0 min-w-0 flex-col overflow-hidden`** so children get a **fixed-height column** and **one** vertical scroll surface (the shell’s `<main>`), not a second competing scroll on the panels wrapper.

**`apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding/src/layouts/AuthenticatedAppShell.tsx`**
- Wrapped **`AppPanelsLayout`** in **`<div className="h-full min-h-0 w-full">`** so height **`100%`** is defined under `AlertDialogProvider` (which does not add a layout node).

**`PANELS_AI_GUIDE.md`**
- Added a short **“PanelsLayout (sewdn)”** section: main slot is a flex column with **`min-h-0`**, avoid **`100svh`** inside it, and put **`overflow-y-auto`** on the app shell’s primary scroll surface.

Onboarding **`bun run build`** passes with these updates.

content.text:
Here is what changed in `DashboardPage.tsx`, aligned with the spacing guidelines (micro → component → section → region) and the patterns in `spacing-guidelines.mdx` / `spacing-radix.stories.tsx`:

### Page frame
- Root: `flex flex-col gap-region` instead of `space-y-8` — **region** rhythm between the welcome block and the grid (per “between top-level page blocks”).
- Grid: `gap-section` instead of `gap-4 md:gap-5` — **section** for gaps between cards/siblings in the layout (same idea as the stat row in the guidelines).

### Welcome header
- `pb-region` under the divider; inner layout `gap-region` on large screens.
- Title block: `flex flex-col gap-micro` so eyebrow, title, and body use **micro** “inside a phrase” spacing.

### Flat cards (`Card` / `CardHeader` / `CardContent`)
- Headers use **`gap-component`** (icon/avatar rows, org block, certification header) and **`pb-0`** so vertical rhythm comes from the card’s built-in **`gap-component`** between header and content, not extra arbitrary padding.
- Title + subtitle stacks: **`gap-micro`** or **`space-y-micro`**.
- **`DashboardNavCard`**: `gap-component` in the header row, **`space-y-micro`** for title/description, **`gap-section`** in `CardContent`.
- Certification main content: **`gap-section`** on the two-column grid; left column uses **`gap-section`** between blocks and **`gap-component`** inside each block; status list **`gap-micro`**; preview list **`gap-component`**; row links **`px-component py-component`**; badge row **`mt-micro`** + **`gap-component`**.
- Certificate teaser: **`p-section`** + **`gap-section`** (inside a panel).
- Finance card: header **`gap-micro`**; body **`gap-section`** between rows; row label/value **`gap-component`**; footer links **`gap-section`**.

### Secondary nav
- Container **`gap-section`**; each link **`gap-micro`** (icon ↔ label), **`px-section py-component`** (section padding horizontally, component vertically — matches “component padding” with a bit more horizontal air on pills).

Semantic classes resolve through `--spacing-*` in `default.css`, so they follow operational vs spacious density without extra logic. Build passes.

content.text:
Here is what changed:

### More air on each flat card
- **`flatCardClass`** now includes **`p-section`** and a light **`bg-muted/10`** (with **`dark:bg-muted/5`**) so each tile has a clear **section**-scale inset on all sides. That overrides the default **`py-component`** on `Card` via `cn`, so vertical padding matches the horizontal token instead of feeling tight.

### No double horizontal padding
- **`flatCardChromeClass`** is **`px-0`** on every flat **`CardHeader`** and **`CardContent`**, so you are not stacking the shell’s **`p-section`** with the primitives’ default **`px-component`**.

### More space between tiles
- The bento grid uses **`gap-region`** instead of **`gap-section`** so cards sit a bit farther apart.

### Accidental truncation fixed
- The **Demo** nav cards, **Design system** card, and **Onderaan in de zijbalk** + **`PROTOTYPE_SECONDARY_NAV`** block were missing after a bad edit; that JSX is restored so the page matches the intended layout again.

Build and lints are clean. If you want the tiles even looser, the next step would be **`p-section md:p-region`** on `flatCardClass` only at `md+` (still token-based).

Here is what was implemented.

### `dashboard-mock-data.ts`

Here’s what was added and how the layout works.

### `dashboard-mock-data.ts`
- **`DashboardNotificationModule`**: `"user-management" | "certification" | "invoicing"`.
- **`DashboardNotification`**: `id`, `module`, `message`, `occurredAt` (ISO).
- **`mockRecentNotifications()`**: five sample rows (user activation, cert approved/submitted, new invoice, user added), sorted newest first.

### `RecentNotificationsWidget.tsx`
- Same **card header** pattern as certificatie/facturen: **`Notification01Icon`** in the **`size-10` / `rounded-xl` / `bg-primary/8`** tile, title **“Meldingen”**, short description.
- **`h4`**: **“Recente meldingen”** (same utility stack as other section labels).
- Each row: **`Item`** `variant="muted"` `size="sm"` with **`ItemMedia`** (module icon from **`navItemByKey`**: gebruikersbeheer, aanvragen, facturen), **`ItemTitle`** = message, **`ItemDescription`** = **`nl-BE`** date + time (`day`, `short` month, `hour`, `minute`).
- **`ItemTitle`** uses **`line-clamp-none`** so longer lines wrap instead of single-line clamp.
- List area: **`min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-y-auto`** so extra items scroll inside the tile on tall bento rows.

### `DashboardPage.tsx` (bento height)
- Grid: **`items-stretch`** so all columns share the row height.
- **Left column** (`lg:col-span-4`): **`flex flex-col gap-region lg:h-full lg:min-h-0`**.
  - **`SessionContextWidget`**: **`shrink-0`** (natural height).
  - **`RecentNotificationsWidget`**: **`min-h-0 flex-1`** (fills the rest of the column so **profile + meldingen** together match the height of the cert and invoice columns).
- **Certificatie** wrapper: **`lg:h-full flex min-h-0`** + widget **`className="min-h-0 w-full flex-1"`**.
- **Facturen**: **`lg:h-full min-h-0`** on the widget (still **`h-full`** on the card).

### `dashboard-widgets/index.ts`
- Re-exports **`RecentNotificationsWidget`** and its props type.

<user_query>
Why are the bento grid widgets currently so large in height?
</user_query>

Checking the dashboard grid and widget height classes to explain what's driving the tall layout.

[REDACTED]

Here’s **why** those tiles end up so tall:

### 1. **The row is as tall as the tallest column**
The bento row is a **12-column grid**. Row height is driven by the **tallest** of the three columns (usually **Certificatie**: header, list, “Actieve certificaten” strip, two links). The grid does **not** shrink each column to its own content; it uses one shared row height.

### 2. **`items-stretch` (and default grid stretch)**
With **`items-stretch`**, every column **stretches vertically** to that shared row height. So the left column and the invoice column get the **same height** as the certification column, even if their own content is shorter.

### 3. **We then fill that height on purpose**
Earlier we aligned the bento by:

- **`lg:h-full`** on the left stack, certification wrapper, and invoice widget so they **fill the grid cell**.
- **`h-full`** on the **Certificatie** and **Facturen** cards (they grow to the full cell).
- **`flex-1 min-h-0`** on **Recent notifications** so it **takes all space under the profile** in the left column.

So the tiles are “large” because **(a)** the row is at least as tall as the certification block, and **(b)** we **stretch** the other cards (and flex the notifications card) to **use that full height** instead of staying content-sized.

---

**If you want shorter tiles:** use **`items-start`** (columns only as tall as their content, heights can differ), and/or drop **`h-full` / `flex-1`** so cards hug their content. That trades the strict “all columns same height” bento for a more compact layout.
